records.title: Received stock becomes sellable inventory
records.intent: A stock receipt increases on-hand and sellable quantity for its SKU, and reservations cannot exceed the derived sellable quantity.
records.given: An empty inventory state and a stock_received event for a SKU and quantity.
records.when: The event is applied and availability is queried at a later timestamp.
records.then: onHand and sellable both increase by the receipt quantity; canReserve is true at or below sellable quantity and false above it.

records.title: Reservations hold stock until expiration
records.intent: A reservation request with sufficient sellable stock creates a held reservation that subtracts from sellable availability until its expiration boundary.
records.given: A SKU has stock on hand and a reservation_requested event includes reservationId, quantity, requestedAt, and expiresAt.
records.when: Availability and reservation status are queried before expiresAt.
records.then: The reservation status is held, held quantity increases, and sellable quantity is reduced by the held amount.

records.title: Order confirmation commits held stock
records.intent: Confirming a held reservation converts it from held to committed stock without returning it to the sellable pool.
records.given: A held reservation exists for a SKU.
records.when: An order_confirmed event is applied before the reservation expires.
records.then: The reservation status becomes confirmed, committed quantity increases, held quantity decreases, and sellable remains reduced by the committed quantity.

records.title: Reservation expiration releases held stock
records.intent: A held reservation is no longer active at its expiresAt boundary and its stock returns to sellable availability.
records.given: A held reservation has an expiresAt timestamp.
records.when: Availability and reservation status are queried at or after expiresAt.
records.then: The reservation status is expired, held quantity is zero for that reservation, and sellable quantity includes the released units.

records.title: Cancellation releases unshipped allocations
records.intent: Canceling a held, confirmed, or backordered reservation makes it inactive and releases any held or committed units back to sellable inventory.
records.given: A reservation is held, confirmed, or backordered and has not shipped.
records.then: The reservation status becomes canceled and availability no longer counts it as held, committed, or backordered.

records.title: Duplicate event IDs are idempotent
records.intent: Applying the same event ID more than once must not duplicate stock, reservations, commitments, shipments, or returns.
records.given: Any lifecycle event has already been processed.
records.when: The exact same event ID is applied again.
records.then: State and derived availability are unchanged by the duplicate event.

records.title: Shipment consumes committed stock
records.intent: Shipping a confirmed reservation removes the committed units from on-hand stock and later cancellation must not restore them.
records.when: An order_shipped event is applied and a later cancellation is attempted.
records.then: The reservation remains shipped, onHand decreases by the shipped quantity, shipped quantity increases, and sellable stock is not inflated by the later cancellation.

records.title: Restock fills backorders FIFO
records.intent: Reservation requests that cannot be fully held become backordered, and later stock receipts allocate full reservations to the oldest backorders first.
records.given: Multiple backordered reservations exist for the same SKU.
records.when: A stock_received event adds fewer units than needed to fill all backorders.
records.then: The oldest fully satisfiable backorder becomes held first; later backorders remain backordered until enough sellable stock exists.

records.title: Returns restore only sellable stock
records.intent: A return for a shipped reservation restores on-hand stock only when the return disposition is sellable; damaged returns are tracked but not sellable.
records.given: A reservation has shipped and an item_returned event includes disposition sellable or damaged.
records.then: Sellable returns increase onHand and returnedSellable; damaged returns increase damagedReturns without increasing onHand or sellable availability.
